verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane-associated matrix-facing component of complex V. However, there is well-documented ectopic surface display of ATP synthase subunits (including beta) on the plasma membrane of multiple cell types — notably hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, and many tumor cells — where it functions as a receptor for apolipoprotein A-I, angiostatin, coupling factor 6, and others. This 'ecto-ATP synthase' / ecto-F1-beta is a recognized cancer cell-surface target with antibody and small-molecule programs (e.g., HUH-7 targeting, angiostatin binding). This qualifies as cell-state/oncogenic-induced ecto-display.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of mitochondrial F1 subunit trafficking to PM outer leaflet remains unclear; surface pool is minor vs dominant mitochondrial localization.
